title: Today's AI Brief: Anthropic's Cowork and the evolving AI landscape
summary: Anthropic launches Cowork, a Claude Desktop agent for non-technical users
imageAlt: Image from source article for Anthropic launches Cowork, a Claude Desktop agent that works in your files — no coding required
imageCaption: Anthropic launches Cowork, a Claude Desktop agent that works in your files — no coding required
imageSourceTitle: Anthropic launches Cowork, a Claude Desktop agent that works in your files — no coding required

body.text: I'm Leo Stone, and welcome to your AI briefing. Today, we're looking at some fresh developments in the world of artificial intelligence. I've got my eyes on Anthropic's new launch, and I'm excited to walk you through it. We're also going to touch on a couple of other interesting AI-related news items that caught my attention.

First up, Anthropic has just released Cowork, a new AI agent capability that brings the power of its Claude Code tool to non-technical users. This is interesting because it means you don't need to know how to code to use it. According to the folks at Anthropic, the team built this feature to make AI more accessible. I'm looking at this as a significant step in making AI tools more user-friendly.

In other news, researchers at Google DeepMind have announced a breakthrough in protein folding prediction. This is a big deal because understanding how proteins fold can lead to major advances in fields like medicine and biotechnology. It's amazing to see how AI is being used to tackle some of the world's toughest scientific challenges.

And finally, I've been reading about the growing trend of AI-powered virtual influencers on social media. These virtual personalities are becoming increasingly popular, and it's raising interesting questions about the future of influencer marketing and the role of AI in our online lives.

My quick read: the artificial intelligence story keeps getting bigger, but also stranger in very human ways. One moment it is strategy and infrastructure; the next, the Vatican is in the room. That is a pretty good sign this technology has left the lab and wandered into the town square.

sources.title: Anthropic launches Cowork, a Claude Desktop agent that works in your files — no coding required
